title: Heart Attack
summary: A heart attack occurs when blood flow to part of the heart is blocked. Every minute without treatment can cause permanent heart damage.
steps: Call emergency services (108/112) immediately — do not wait.
Help the person sit or lie in a comfortable position (semi-upright is best).
Loosen any tight clothing around the neck, chest, or waist.
If the person is conscious and not allergic, give them 300mg aspirin to chew slowly.
Keep the person calm and still — reassure them help is coming.
Monitor their breathing and pulse continuously.
If the person becomes unconscious and stops breathing — begin CPR immediately.
Use an AED (Automated Defibrillator) if available and follow its instructions.
what_to_do: Keep the person still and calm
Give aspirin (300mg) if available and not contraindicated
Prepare to perform CPR if they become unresponsive
what_not_to_do: Do NOT leave the person alone
Do NOT allow the person to walk or exert themselves
Do NOT give aspirin if allergic or if already on blood thinners without guidance
Do NOT apply pressure to the chest manually
Do NOT give food or water
when_to_call_ambulance: Immediately when you suspect a heart attack
Chest pain, tightness, or pressure lasting more than a few minutes
Pain spreading to the arm, neck, jaw, or back
Shortness of breath, cold sweat, nausea, or lightheadedness
